mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly boosting the present value of equity cash flows and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5 percent within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor market conditions that triggers recession fears, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns during historical recessions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and consumer credit outstanding indicate a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint decline in INDPRO and RSAFS fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 40 percent of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: During active monetary tightening cycles, rising consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases fail to predict negative 20-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of observations during identified tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: When new housing starts fall below 1.2 million units annually, it signals a sharp contraction in business and consumer confidence, triggering a risk-off rotation that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts below 1.2M fail to precede negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic momentum that supports corporate earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month PAYEMS changes fail to predict positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production rises while consumer credit outstanding falls indicates a supply-driven growth phase that eventually leads to inventory gluts and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The specified divergence pattern fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 45 percent of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery phases, accelerating real GDP growth combined with stable unemployment drives earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Accelerating GDPC1 fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of identified recovery periods.
